name: Proton-M
first: Soviet-era heavy launcher — flew every Soviet Mars + Luna mission and Russia's ISS modules
description: Khrunichev-built four-stage hypergolic launcher in service since 1965 (M variant from 2001). Notable interplanetary missions: Mars 2 / 3 (1971), Luna 17 / Lunokhod 1 (1970), Luna 24 (1976), Vega (1984), Phobos 1 / 2 (1988), Mars 96 (1996, lost). Last commercial launch in 2023; being phased out in favour of Angara-A5.
